lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Can I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you do here?

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Well, I can cure plain cowhides into pieces of leather ready for crafting.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: I work with ordinary, hard or dragonhide leather and also snakeskin. What would you like to know about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ordinary leather.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: I can work one piece of cow hide into ordinary leather. It only costs one piece of gold.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hard leather.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Hard leather is made by specially treating normal leather by wetting and drying several times.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: It's then treated with pine oils. It's harder wearing than ordinary leather.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Snake skins.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: I can work one piece of snake hide into a snakeskin. It only costs 15 pieces of gold. Snakeskin armour is great for intermediate level rangers and provides more defence than hard and studded leather.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dragonhide leather.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Dragon leather is made from dragonhides. One piece of hide can be worked into one piece of dragon leather. It costs 20 coins each piece.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Dragon leather is hard-wearing and has excellent flexibility. You can make strong and supple armour with this.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm bored of leather already.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: You may be bored now, but an arrow in the backside will soon fix that!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Leatherworker
lines.text: Suit yourself.
